result.summary: Skyia is a React/Vite frontend with a PHP/MySQL backend, featuring an immersive terminal-like UI for AI interactions. The project includes model benchmarking, user authentication, and a credit-based progression system. The backend handles API keys and database operations, while the frontend manages UI state and user interactions.
result.suitability: Mistral can assist with planning and reviewing the proposed feature but cannot directly implement changes due to policy constraints. Recommendations will focus on API fields, DB columns, frontend placement, and potential risks.

proposed_actions.title: Add dual mode model standings (wins/losses by role)
proposed_actions.why: Enhances competitive tracking and user engagement by providing detailed performance metrics for different roles.

proposed_actions.title: Implement archived_at for finished dual reports
proposed_actions.why: Improves data management by marking completed reports, aiding in historical tracking and cleanup.

proposed_actions.title: Add text quality audit for saved report payloads
proposed_actions.why: Ensures data integrity by detecting mojibake, replacement characters, or control characters in saved reports.

files_to_create.reason: Adds necessary database columns for tracking wins/losses by role and marking archived reports.
Provides a utility function to audit text quality in report payloads.

files_to_update.instructions: Update the model stats endpoint to include wins/losses by role in responses and handle archived_at for dual reports.
files_to_update.reason: Extends the backend to support updating dual mode standings.

files_to_update.instructions: Add a section to display dual mode standings and handle archived reports.
files_to_update.reason: Enhances the frontend to display dual mode standings in the end game report.

risks: Adding JSON columns for wins/losses by role may require careful handling in the database to avoid schema conflicts.
The text quality audit may introduce performance overhead if not optimized for large payloads.
Ensuring backward compatibility with existing reports and stats is critical to avoid data loss or corruption.
questions_for_user: Should the dual mode standings be reset or initialized with default values for existing models?
Are there specific roles or categories that need to be predefined for the dual mode standings?
What is the expected behavior for archived reports in terms of retrieval and display?
